The system is for emergency room intake.
The system prioritizes patients based on clinical urgency.
The system ensures critical cases are surfaced to medical staff immediately.
The system ensures the waiting queue always reflects current patient conditions.
The system operates in a high-pressure environment.
The system supports registering incoming patients with their name.
The system supports registering incoming patients with their presenting symptoms.
The system assigns each patient an urgency level from 1 to 5.
Urgency level 1 is critical.
Urgency level 5 is non-urgent.
The urgency level is based on the symptoms.
The queue is ordered by urgency level first.
The queue is ordered by arrival time within the same urgency level.
The queue is reordered immediately when a new patient arrives.
The queue is reordered immediately when an existing patient is re-triaged to a different urgency level.
The system lets a physician take the patient at the head of the queue.
Taking a patient removes them from the queue.
The system shows a live dashboard of the queue.
The dashboard shows each patient's urgency.
The dashboard shows each patient's waiting time.
An emergency department triage system that ranks patients by how urgently they need care.
It continuously monitors and rearranges the waiting list so the most critical cases are seen first.
Medical staff can instantly see which patients require immediate attention, helping them act quickly.
When a new patient arrives or a condition changes, the queue updates in real time to reflect current urgency.
This tool organizes patient flow in a busy ER by prioritizing clinical need over order of arrival.
The system records each incoming patient's name and the symptoms they report.
It captures the chief complaint and identity of every person who walks in.
Staff enter a patient's details and presenting issues into the intake module.
Registration logs the individual's identity along with their stated medical concerns.
Users provide personal information and symptom descriptions to begin the triage process.
Based on the reported symptoms, the system calculates an urgency score from 1 (critical) to 5 (non-urgent).
It assigns a triage level between one and five according to the severity of the patient's condition.
Clinical urgency is determined automatically from the symptoms entered during registration.
Each person receives a priority rating that reflects how quickly they need medical intervention.
The tool maps symptom data to a five-level urgency scale to categorize patient acuity.
The queue sorts patients primarily by their urgency level, and secondarily by arrival time within that level.
It orders the waiting list so that higher-priority cases appear before lower-priority ones, with ties broken by time of entry.
Patients with the same triage rating are sequenced based on who arrived first.
The waiting line is arranged first by clinical priority, then chronologically among equal-priority individuals.
When two patients share the same urgency, the one who has been waiting longer moves ahead.
The queue reorders instantly whenever a new patient registers or an existing patient's urgency changes.
Any admission or re-triage event triggers an immediate resort of the waiting list.
If a patient's condition worsens and their level is updated, the system re-sorts the queue without delay.
The waiting line dynamically adjusts in real time as new patients enter or existing ones are reclassified.
Changes in patient status cause the queue to be refreshed immediately to reflect the latest triage information.
A physician can take the next patient from the front of the queue, removing that person from the waiting list.
When a doctor is ready to see a patient, they pull the person at the top of the list, which clears them from the queue.
The system allows a clinician to select the highest-priority patient, who is then removed from the active waiting line.
Medical staff can pick the patient who is first in line, which deletes that entry from the queue.
The head of the queue is taken by a healthcare provider, and that patient is no longer shown as waiting.
A live dashboard displays the current queue, showing each patient's urgency level and how long they have been waiting.
Staff can view a real-time screen that lists all waiting patients with their triage score and elapsed wait time.
The interface provides an up-to-date visual of the waiting list, including priority ratings and duration of wait.
Users see a dynamic board that updates automatically with each patient's acuity and the time they have spent in the queue.
The dashboard presents a live feed of the queue, indicating both clinical priority and waiting duration for every individual.
